children.text: You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 1–13, which are based on Reading Passage 1 below.
Problems in the Australian sugar industry

The Australian town of Mossman in the state of Queensland sits in a tropical landscape between the rainforest and the Great Barrier Reef. Eco-tourism is important there; more than 80 per cent of Douglas Shire, of which Mossman is the administrative centre, is protected by World Heritage listing. But for most of the town's history, forest and reef have been largely irrelevant: since the sugar mill was built in 1894, the town has relied on sugarcane. Now Mossman is holding its breath. For two years the mill used by all the farms has been close to bankruptcy. It is at the centre of the economic shocks that have shaken Australia's sugar industry, and for lifetime farmers and a long list of cane-industry workers a way of life will disappear if the mill closes. Mossman has roughly 160 growers, who now produce less than one million tonnes of the nation's annual cane harvest of 30–40 million tonnes. But it is a microcosm of the industry. All across Australia, the cane-growing business is being squeezed between the pincers of economics and the environment.

The ten-year average return to sugar growers throughout the 1990s was about $350 a tonne. In early 2004, sugar prices plummeted, resulting in a 25-year-low average price for Australian sugar of about $232 a tonne. Although figures vary widely across farms and regions, that was about what it cost to grow a tonne of sugar in Australia. To forestall social and economic disaster, the Government offered more than $400 million to encourage growers to leave the industry. By the end of the year, 21 farmers had taken up the offer to leave, but another 1,000 are thought to be seriously considering it, allowing those remaining to buy the vacated land and improve their economies of scale.

Fourth-generation Mossman grower Bill Phillips-Turner is one who plans to fight on. 'The consequences of losing the mill would be catastrophic,' Bill says. 'Sugar has a big economic multiplier effect: for every dollar generated from sugar, an additional $7 is generated in the wider community. Because of limited options around here, most people now employed by the industry would have to leave the area to find work.' The farmer-shareholders have so far saved the mill by accepting substantial cuts to cane payments, but this has come at a big cost to everyone. As chairman of the board of the mill, Bill has presided over some tough and unpopular decisions; the hardest was slashing the mill workforce. Assets were sold and maintenance costs cut. The board has also worked hard to find new ways of doing business. Ethanol production, where sugar is used to produce fuel, has potential, and co-generation, using cane waste to generate electric power, is another possibility. However, the most radical—but preferred—alternative is to create a future for the mill as a food factory, turning out quality sugar-based foods.

In addition to the economic struggle, there is the environmental one. The sugar industry has the reputation of being environmentally damaging, but it has some surprising supporters. Douglas Shire mayor, Mike Berwick, is a well-known environmentalist and might be expected to be anti-cane. ‘There's no question of the past damage it's done to the reef through chemical and nutrient run-off*,' he says. 'But there's a formula for sustainable cane production and Mossman has nearly reached it.' Another surprise endorsement for cane comes from the Queensland Environmental Protection Agency's sugar liaison officer, Karen Benn. 'I'm less worried about the effect of cane on the environment than I am about other agriculture,' she says. 'There are good growers everywhere, but at Mossman they seem to have taken up the challenges faster.' For example, sediment run-off, previously one of the main environmental problems caused by cane growing, is now nothing like it once was, according to Dr Brian Roberts, co-ordinator of the Douglas Shire Water Quality Improvement Program. ‘North Queensland used to hold the record for soil loss,' he says. 'Now ... cane country is accumulating soil.'

However, these improvements have been achieved at a great cost to growers. Now in his 60s, Tom Watters has spent a lifetime on the same farm. Fourteen years ago, he was alerted to the effect his cane could be having on Mackay Creek, the narrow waterway that receives all his run-off, and so Tom planted a 5,000-tree buffer along the edge of the creek, built up the edges of the creek with rocks to prevent erosion, and began exploring farming methods that cause minimal soil disturbance. However, none of these costly initiatives has helped him get better cane prices. Another cane grower, Doug Crees, comments that 'Economically, it [cane growing] doesn't make much sense. But there's more to life than money.' It's this addiction to the way of life that keeps many cane farmers growing an underperforming crop. 'It's a good lifestyle,' Doug says. 'I spend eight months working on the farm and four months looking after our kids while my wife works in town. I've been looking at alternative crops, like forestry and cocoa, and it turns out that working away from the farm is the best diversification we could do. However, I still don't want to do that.'

It is difficult to see how anyone can deal satisfactorily with the passing of a way of life. Cane farmers have been part of eastern Queensland for more than a century, but despite the efforts they have put into fighting the good environmental fight, there is no guarantee that the new way of life evolving there will include cane.
*run-off: water that is used for agriculture and then passes into waterways, carrying soil and agricultural chemicals with it
Look at the following statements (Questions 1–4) and the list of people below.
Match each statement with the correct person,
, in boxes 1–4 on your answer sheet.
Mossman cane-farming practices are close to an environmentally friendly model.
Financial return is not the only important factor for cane growers.
Cane sugar may not harm the environment as much as other crops do.
The local population would decline if the sugar-processing plant closed.

explanationSection.sectionTitle: 1. 人物匹配（Questions 1–4：Match each statement with the correct person, A–F）
explanationSection.text:
题目翻译：莫斯曼的甘蔗种植方式接近环保模式。
答案：B（Mike Berwick，迈克・伯威克）
解析：定位 Paragraph 4 中 “Douglas Shire mayor, Mike Berwick... ‘There’s no question of the past damage it’s done to the reef through chemical and nutrient run-off. But there’s a formula for sustainable cane production and Mossman has nearly reached it’”，“sustainable cane production” 对应题干 “environmentally friendly model”，“nearly reached it” 对应 “close to”，明确提到迈克・伯威克认为莫斯曼的甘蔗种植接近可持续（环保）模式，因此答案为 B。
题目翻译：经济回报并非甘蔗种植户唯一的重要考量因素。
答案：F（Doug Crees，道格・克里斯）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “Another cane grower, Doug Crees, comments that ‘Economically, it \[cane growing\] doesn’t make much sense. But there’s more to life than money’”，明确提到道格认为 “生活不只是为了钱”，即经济回报并非唯一重要因素，因此答案为 F。
题目翻译：甘蔗对环境的危害可能不如其他作物大。
答案：C（Karen Benn，凯伦・本）
解析：定位 Paragraph 4 中 “Queensland Environmental Protection Agency’s sugar liaison officer, Karen Benn. ‘I’m less worried about the effect of cane on the environment than I am about other agriculture’”，“less worried about... than...” 对应题干 “may not harm... as much as...”，明确提到凯伦・本认为甘蔗对环境的危害小于其他农业作物，因此答案为 C。
题目翻译：若糖厂关闭，当地人口将减少。
答案：A（Bill Phillips-Turner，比尔・菲利普斯 - 特纳）
解析：定位 Paragraph 3 中 “Fourth-generation Mossman grower Bill Phillips-Turner... ‘... most people now employed by the industry would have to leave the area to find work’”，“leave the area” 对应题干 “population would decline”，明确提到比尔认为糖厂关闭会导致从业者离开，当地人口减少，因此答案为 A。

items.text:
（1）题目 1：Mossman cane-farming practices are close to an environmentally friendly model.
题目翻译：莫斯曼的甘蔗种植方式接近环保模式。
答案：B（Mike Berwick，迈克・伯威克）
解析：定位 Paragraph 4 中 “Douglas Shire mayor, Mike Berwick... ‘There’s no question of the past damage it’s done to the reef through chemical and nutrient run-off. But there’s a formula for sustainable cane production and Mossman has nearly reached it’”，“sustainable cane production” 对应题干 “environmentally friendly model”，“nearly reached it” 对应 “close to”，明确提到迈克・伯威克认为莫斯曼的甘蔗种植接近可持续（环保）模式，因此答案为 B。

（2）题目 2：Financial return is not the only important factor for cane growers.
题目翻译：经济回报并非甘蔗种植户唯一的重要考量因素。
答案：F（Doug Crees，道格・克里斯）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “Another cane grower, Doug Crees, comments that ‘Economically, it \[cane growing\] doesn’t make much sense. But there’s more to life than money’”，明确提到道格认为 “生活不只是为了钱”，即经济回报并非唯一重要因素，因此答案为 F。

（3）题目 3：Cane sugar may not harm the environment as much as other crops do.
题目翻译：甘蔗对环境的危害可能不如其他作物大。
答案：C（Karen Benn，凯伦・本）
解析：定位 Paragraph 4 中 “Queensland Environmental Protection Agency’s sugar liaison officer, Karen Benn. ‘I’m less worried about the effect of cane on the environment than I am about other agriculture’”，“less worried about... than...” 对应题干 “may not harm... as much as...”，明确提到凯伦・本认为甘蔗对环境的危害小于其他农业作物，因此答案为 C。

（4）题目 4：The local population would decline if the sugar-processing plant closed.
题目翻译：若糖厂关闭，当地人口将减少。
答案：A（Bill Phillips-Turner，比尔・菲利普斯 - 特纳）
解析：定位 Paragraph 3 中 “Fourth-generation Mossman grower Bill Phillips-Turner... ‘... most people now employed by the industry would have to leave the area to find work’”，“leave the area” 对应题干 “population would decline”，明确提到比尔认为糖厂关闭会导致从业者离开，当地人口减少，因此答案为 A。

children.text: In the first paragraph, the writer says that the town is ‘holding its breath’ because
B its tourism business is threatened.
C most of its people have left the town.
D a key processing plant may shut down.
According to the writer, cane growers who refuse the Government offer are expected to
B sell their land at a low price.
C find jobs in other industries.
D seek financial help from banks.
Which of the following did Bill Phillips-Turner find most difficult to do?
D cut the number of mill staff
Cane grower Doug Crees says that he
A would prefer to grow cocoa.
B wants to remain on his farm.
C wants his family to live together.
D will look for part-time work in town.

explanationSection.sectionTitle: 2. 选择题（Questions 5–8：Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D）
explanationSection.text:
题目翻译：第一段中，作者提到小镇 “屏住呼吸”，原因是_\___\__
答案：D（a key processing plant may shut down，一家关键加工厂可能关闭）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “Now Mossman is holding its breath. For two years the mill used by all the farms has been close to bankruptcy... a way of life will disappear if the mill closes”，“holding its breath” 体现小镇的焦虑，核心原因是 “糖厂（关键加工厂）濒临破产，可能关闭”，与选项 D 一致，因此答案为 D。
题目翻译：根据作者的说法，拒绝政府补贴的甘蔗种植户预计会_\___\__
答案：A（expand their farms，扩大农场规模）
解析：定位 Paragraph 2 中 “By the end of the year, 21 farmers had taken up the offer to leave, but another 1 000 are thought to be seriously considering it, allowing those remaining to buy the vacated land and improve their economies of scale”，“those remaining” 对应题干 “growers who refuse the Government offer”，“buy the vacated land” 对应 “expand their farms”，明确提到留下的种植户会收购土地、扩大规模，与选项 A 一致，因此答案为 A。
题目翻译：比尔・菲利普斯 - 特纳认为以下哪件事最难做？
答案：D（cut the number of mill staff，裁减糖厂员工数量）
解析：定位 Paragraph 3 中 “As chairman of the board of the mill, Bill has presided over some tough and unpopular decisions; the hardest was slashing the mill workforce”，“the hardest” 对应题干 “find most difficult to do”，“slashing the mill workforce” 对应 “cut the number of mill staff”，明确提到比尔最难做的是裁减员工，与选项 D 一致，因此答案为 D。
题目翻译：甘蔗种植户道格・克里斯表示他_\___\__
答案：B（wants to remain on his farm，希望留在自己的农场）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “Doug Crees... ‘I’ve been looking at alternative crops, like forestry and cocoa, and it turns out that working away from the farm is the best diversification we could do. However, I still don’t want to do that’”，“working away from the farm” 是替代方案，“don’t want to do that” 体现他 “希望留在农场”，与选项 B 一致，因此答案为 B。

items.text:
（1）题目 5：In the first paragraph, the writer says that the town is ‘holding its breath’ because
题目翻译：第一段中，作者提到小镇 “屏住呼吸”，原因是_\___\__
答案：D（a key processing plant may shut down，一家关键加工厂可能关闭）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “Now Mossman is holding its breath. For two years the mill used by all the farms has been close to bankruptcy... a way of life will disappear if the mill closes”，“holding its breath” 体现小镇的焦虑，核心原因是 “糖厂（关键加工厂）濒临破产，可能关闭”，与选项 D 一致，因此答案为 D。

（2）题目 6：According to the writer, cane growers who refuse the Government offer are expected to
题目翻译：根据作者的说法，拒绝政府补贴的甘蔗种植户预计会_\___\__
答案：A（expand their farms，扩大农场规模）
解析：定位 Paragraph 2 中 “By the end of the year, 21 farmers had taken up the offer to leave, but another 1 000 are thought to be seriously considering it, allowing those remaining to buy the vacated land and improve their economies of scale”，“those remaining” 对应题干 “growers who refuse the Government offer”，“buy the vacated land” 对应 “expand their farms”，明确提到留下的种植户会收购土地、扩大规模，与选项 A 一致，因此答案为 A。

（3）题目 7：Which of the following did Bill Phillips-Turner find most difficult to do?
题目翻译：比尔・菲利普斯 - 特纳认为以下哪件事最难做？
答案：D（cut the number of mill staff，裁减糖厂员工数量）
解析：定位 Paragraph 3 中 “As chairman of the board of the mill, Bill has presided over some tough and unpopular decisions; the hardest was slashing the mill workforce”，“the hardest” 对应题干 “find most difficult to do”，“slashing the mill workforce” 对应 “cut the number of mill staff”，明确提到比尔最难做的是裁减员工，与选项 D 一致，因此答案为 D。

（4）题目 8：Cane grower Doug Crees says that he
题目翻译：甘蔗种植户道格・克里斯表示他_\___\__
答案：B（wants to remain on his farm，希望留在自己的农场）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “Doug Crees... ‘I’ve been looking at alternative crops, like forestry and cocoa, and it turns out that working away from the farm is the best diversification we could do. However, I still don’t want to do that’”，“working away from the farm” 是替代方案，“don’t want to do that” 体现他 “希望留在农场”，与选项 B 一致，因此答案为 B。

children.text: Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 1?
In boxes 9–13 on your answer sheet, write
if the statement agrees with the information
if the statement contradicts the information
if there is no information on this
In 2004, the cost of producing sugar in Australia was similar to the selling price.
Farmers who accepted the Government offer have mostly moved to the city.
Fuel production is regarded as the most desirable alternative business for the sugar mill.
Tom Watters reduced his use of pesticides.
Environmentally friendly farming practices have been profitable for Tom Watters.

explanationSection.text:
题目翻译：2004 年，澳大利亚甘蔗的生产成本与售价相近。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 2 中 “In early 2004... average price for Australian sugar of about $232 a tonne. Although figures vary widely across farms and regions, that was about what it cost to grow a tonne of sugar in Australia”，“that”指售价$232 / 吨，“what it cost” 指生产成本，明确提到两者 “相近”，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。
题目翻译：接受政府补贴的种植户大多已搬到城市。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：Paragraph 2 仅提到 “21 farmers had taken up the offer to leave”（21 名种植户接受补贴退出），但未说明他们 “搬到城市”，无相关信息，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。
题目翻译：燃料生产被视为糖厂最理想的替代业务。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 3 中 “Ethanol production, where sugar is used to produce fuel, has potential... However, the most radical-but preferred-alternative is to create a future for the mill as a food factory”，“ethanol production” 对应 “fuel production”，明确提到 “首选替代方案是食品厂”，而非燃料生产，与题干描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。
题目翻译：汤姆・沃特斯减少了杀虫剂的使用。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “Tom planted a 5 000-tree buffer... built up the edges of the creek with rocks... began exploring farming methods that cause minimal soil disturbance”，仅提到汤姆采取的环保措施（种植缓冲树、加固溪岸、减少土壤扰动），但未提及 “减少杀虫剂使用”，无相关信息，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。
题目翻译：环保种植方式为汤姆・沃特斯带来了收益。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “However, none of these costly initiatives has helped him get better cane prices”，“these costly initiatives” 指环保措施，明确提到环保措施未帮助他获得更高价格，即 “未带来收益”，与题干描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。

items.text:
（1）题目 9：In 2004, the cost of producing sugar in Australia was similar to the selling price.
题目翻译：2004 年，澳大利亚甘蔗的生产成本与售价相近。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 2 中 “In early 2004... average price for Australian sugar of about $232 a tonne. Although figures vary widely across farms and regions, that was about what it cost to grow a tonne of sugar in Australia”，“that”指售价$232 / 吨，“what it cost” 指生产成本，明确提到两者 “相近”，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。

（2）题目 10：Farmers who accepted the Government offer have mostly moved to the city.
题目翻译：接受政府补贴的种植户大多已搬到城市。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：Paragraph 2 仅提到 “21 farmers had taken up the offer to leave”（21 名种植户接受补贴退出），但未说明他们 “搬到城市”，无相关信息，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。

（3）题目 11：Fuel production is regarded as the most desirable alternative business for the sugar mill.
题目翻译：燃料生产被视为糖厂最理想的替代业务。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 3 中 “Ethanol production, where sugar is used to produce fuel, has potential... However, the most radical-but preferred-alternative is to create a future for the mill as a food factory”，“ethanol production” 对应 “fuel production”，明确提到 “首选替代方案是食品厂”，而非燃料生产，与题干描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。

（4）题目 12：Tom Watters reduced his use of pesticides.
题目翻译：汤姆・沃特斯减少了杀虫剂的使用。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “Tom planted a 5 000-tree buffer... built up the edges of the creek with rocks... began exploring farming methods that cause minimal soil disturbance”，仅提到汤姆采取的环保措施（种植缓冲树、加固溪岸、减少土壤扰动），但未提及 “减少杀虫剂使用”，无相关信息，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。

（5）题目 13：Environmentally friendly farming practices have been profitable for Tom Watters.
题目翻译：环保种植方式为汤姆・沃特斯带来了收益。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “However, none of these costly initiatives has helped him get better cane prices”，“these costly initiatives” 指环保措施，明确提到环保措施未帮助他获得更高价格，即 “未带来收益”，与题干描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。
